title: CholecT50 reasoning - clinician review
intro_md:
Watch each clip, then rate the model's **answer** and its three-level **reasoning** `<think>`.

The reasoning is always written in three labelled levels (**L1 -> L2 -> L3**); what each level should contain depends on the task. A reminder of the levels **for the current task** stays pinned under the video on every item, so you can rate against it as you read.
intro_md_zh:
观看每个片段，然后评价模型的**回答**及其三层**推理** `<think>`。

推理始终按三个带标签的层级书写（**L1 -> L2 -> L3**）；每个层级应包含的内容取决于任务。**当前任务**对应的层级说明会固定显示在每个条目的视频下方，便于您边阅读边评价。

TAL.q: when does a given phase happen?

levels.label: phase signature & onset cue
levels.desc: Names the queried phase and its visual hallmark - the specific instruments, actions and anatomy that define it - and pinpoints the moment it first becomes visible (its onset), grounded in what is on screen rather than a guess.

levels.label: boundary contrast
levels.desc: Distinguishes the phase from the activity immediately before and after it, using the change in instruments/actions to fix where the phase ends (its offset) - so both edges of the window are justified by observation, not just the middle.

levels.label: phase-order bound & conclusion
levels.desc: Places the phase within the fixed cholecystectomy phase order to confirm the window is consistent with the workflow, then states the final time span as the conclusion.

levels.label: current step at the clip's end
levels.desc: From the activity in the FINAL part of the clip (not the early-clip activity), identifies the phase currently in progress by its instruments, action and structures.

levels.label: completion cue
levels.desc: Gives the observed evidence that the current phase's objective is essentially achieved (target divided / freed / packed / fossa cleared), so that a transition to the next phase is due.

levels.label: procedural logic & conclusion
levels.desc: Applies the fixed phase order - the step following the current one is the next action - to name the predicted next phase, or justifies a non-textbook successor from the observed state (e.g. residual bleeding -> coagulation revisited).

levels.label: scan & enumerate
levels.desc: Sweeping the clip from start to end, lists which candidate phases appear and in what order, by their visual signatures; phases that never appear are omitted.

levels.label: boundaries
levels.desc: For each adjacent pair of segments, states the transition cue (the change in instrument/action) that fixes the boundary, so the segments are contiguous and non-overlapping.

levels.label: per-segment content & conclusion
levels.desc: For each segment in order, gives the key instrument-action-structure its caption must carry, then concludes with the list of [start, end] segments and their headline captions.

levels.label: survey the arc
levels.desc: Surveys the clip from start to end, naming the phases/activities it moves through and the overall direction of the procedure.

levels.label: salience & transitions
levels.desc: Separates the salient through-line (the defining actions) from minor or repeated maneuvers that can be compressed, and marks where the main transitions occur.

levels.label: compress & conclusion
levels.desc: Compresses the arc into the high-level summary the final answer states - the condensed account of what the surgeon accomplishes across the clip.

do: Keep exactly three levels (L1 -> L2 -> L3), in order; ground every statement in observed evidence (instruments, actions, tissue/structures, timing).

Use precise vocabulary: instruments (grasper, hook, scissors, clipper, bipolar, irrigator), the seven phases, the Calot (hepatocystic) triangle, anatomy (cystic duct, cystic artery, gallbladder, liver bed/fossa).
Write as if you are watching the video; make L3 conclude the answer; be concise.
Justify the span from the onset/offset activity; do NOT merely restate the time.
Timestamps ARE allowed (the span is the answer).
dont: Do not reveal the facts were provided: never write "given", "as provided", "the label", "the answer", "ground truth", and do not refer to "the captions/observations/text".
Do not invent anything not observed (no patient history, no off-screen events, no measurements).
Do not skip, merge, reorder, or add a level.
Do not hedge or list alternatives ("possibly X or Y") — commit to the reasoning that reaches the answer.
Do not invent a span for an absent phase — conclude it does not occur.

do: Keep exactly three levels (L1 -> L2 -> L3), in order; ground every statement in observed evidence (instruments, actions, tissue/structures, timing).

Use precise vocabulary: instruments (grasper, hook, scissors, clipper, bipolar, irrigator), the seven phases, the Calot (hepatocystic) triangle, anatomy (cystic duct, cystic artery, gallbladder, liver bed/fossa).
Write as if you are watching the video; make L3 conclude the answer; be concise.
Read the END of the clip to set the current phase.
dont: Do not reveal the facts were provided: never write "given", "as provided", "the label", "the answer", "ground truth", and do not refer to "the captions/observations/text".
Do not invent anything not observed (no patient history, no off-screen events, no measurements).
Do not skip, merge, reorder, or add a level.
Do not hedge or list alternatives ("possibly X or Y") — commit to the reasoning that reaches the answer.
Do not base the current phase on early-clip activity.

Write as if you are watching the video; make L3 conclude the answer; be concise.
Keep L3 at HEADLINE level (one short phrase per segment).
Justify non-overlap/contiguity at L2.
Timestamps ARE allowed.
dont: Do not reveal the facts were provided: never write "given", "as provided", "the label", "the answer", "ground truth", and do not refer to "the captions/observations/text".
Do not invent anything not observed (no patient history, no off-screen events, no measurements).
Do not skip, merge, reorder, or add a level.
Do not hedge or list alternatives ("possibly X or Y") — commit to the reasoning that reaches the answer.
Do NOT write the full captions inside the think — that duplicates the answer; headline only.
Do not include phases that are absent (it is a filter).

Use precise vocabulary: instruments (grasper, hook, scissors, clipper, bipolar, irrigator), the seven phases, the Calot (hepatocystic) triangle, anatomy (cystic duct, cystic artery, gallbladder, liver bed/fossa).
Write as if you are watching the video; make L3 conclude the answer; be concise.
Select and compress (arc + salience).
Lowercase instrument names; observable steps only.

Do not skip, merge, reorder, or add a level.

Do NOT enumerate per segment or include timestamps (that is DVC, not VS).
Do not use 'indicates' / 'suggesting that' phrasing.
Do not restate the whole summary inside L1/L2.

TAL.context:
**Temporal Action Localization (TAL).** The model watched this clip and was asked *when a specific surgical phase occurs*. It had to answer with a time span (start-end, in seconds) and justify that span with a three-level reasoning trace. You are validating two things: whether the **answer (the time span) is correct**, and whether the **reasoning genuinely supports it** from what is visible in the video.

**What you can edit:** the model's reasoning and the prompt's DO/DON'T rules are editable here (the time-span answer is read-only for this task). Every edit you make is saved with your response.
TAL.context_zh:
**时间动作定位（TAL）。** 模型观看了该片段，并被要求判断*某个特定手术阶段在何时发生*。它需要给出一个时间区间（起止，以秒为单位），并用三层推理加以论证。您需要验证两点：**答案（时间区间）是否正确**，以及**推理是否真正依据视频中可见的内容支持该答案**。

**可编辑内容：** 此处可编辑模型的推理以及提示词的应当/不应当规则（本任务的时间区间答案为只读）。您的所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
steps: Play the clip and scrub to the highlighted window and its start/end edges.
Read the question and the model's answer (the time span), and decide whether its start and end match what you actually see.
Read the model's reasoning against the pinned guide (L1 visible onset cue, L2 boundary contrast, L3 phase-order check), then open the 'Prompt rules' panel and check it obeys the DO / DON'T.

Rate every question below and add comments where they help. You can edit the reasoning and the DO / DON'T text directly ('Reset to original' undoes); the time-span answer is read-only, so note any answer error in a comment. Every edit is saved with your response.
steps_zh: 播放片段，并拖动到高亮区间及其起止边界处。
阅读问题和模型的答案（时间区间），判断其起点和终点是否与您实际看到的相符。
对照固定显示的层级说明阅读模型的推理（L1 可见的起始线索，L2 边界对比，L3 阶段顺序确认），然后展开「提示词规则」面板，检查其是否遵守应当/不应当。
对下方每个问题评分并在需要时添加备注。您可以直接编辑推理以及应当/不应当文本（点击「恢复原文」可撤销）；时间区间答案为只读，如有答案错误请在备注中说明。所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。

NAP.context:
**Next Action Prediction (NAP).** The model watched this clip and was asked to *predict the next surgical phase* that follows after the clip ends. It had to name that next phase and justify it with a three-level reasoning trace. You are validating whether the **predicted next phase is correct**, and whether the **reasoning reads the END of the clip correctly** to get there.

**What you can edit:** the model's reasoning and the prompt's DO/DON'T rules are editable here (the predicted-phase answer is read-only for this task). Every edit you make is saved with your response.
NAP.context_zh:
**下一步动作预测（NAP）。** 模型观看了该片段，并被要求*预测片段结束后接下来的手术阶段*。它需要指出下一阶段，并用三层推理加以论证。您需要验证**预测的下一阶段是否正确**，以及**推理是否正确解读了片段的结尾**来得出该结论。

**可编辑内容：** 此处可编辑模型的推理以及提示词的应当/不应当规则（本任务的预测阶段答案为只读）。您的所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
steps: Play the clip, paying close attention to the FINAL seconds (the phase in progress at the end).
Read the question and the model's predicted next phase, and decide whether it is correct given the clip's ending state.

Read the model's reasoning (L1 phase in progress at the end, L2 evidence its objective is essentially complete, L3 procedural-order logic), then open the 'Prompt rules' panel and check it obeys the DO / DON'T (read the current phase from the END, not early-clip activity).

Rate every question below and add comments where they help. You can edit the reasoning and the DO / DON'T text directly ('Reset to original' undoes); the predicted-phase answer is read-only, so note any answer error in a comment. Every edit is saved with your response.
steps_zh: 播放片段，特别留意最后几秒（结尾处正在进行的阶段）。
阅读问题和模型预测的下一阶段，根据片段结尾的状态判断其是否正确。
阅读模型的推理（L1 片段结尾正在进行的阶段，L2 该阶段目标基本完成的证据，L3 通向下一阶段的流程顺序逻辑），然后展开「提示词规则」面板，检查其是否遵守应当/不应当（当前阶段必须依据结尾判断，而非片段早期的操作）。
对下方每个问题评分并在需要时添加备注。您可以直接编辑推理以及应当/不应当文本（点击「恢复原文」可撤销）；预测阶段答案为只读，如有答案错误请在备注中说明。所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。

DVC.context:
**Dense Video Captioning (DVC).** The model watched this clip and was asked to *break it into phase segments* - for each segment give a [start, end] time and a short description - then justify the segmentation with a three-level reasoning trace. You are validating whether the **segments, their boundaries, and their descriptions are correct**, and whether the reasoning supports them.

**What you can edit:** the model's answer (the segment list), its reasoning, and the prompt's DO/DON'T rules are all editable here. Every edit you make is saved with your response.
DVC.context_zh:
**密集视频描述（DVC）。** 模型观看了该片段，并被要求*将其划分为多个阶段时间段*——为每段给出 [起始, 结束] 时间和简短描述——并用三层推理论证该划分。您需要验证**各时间段、其边界及描述是否正确**，以及推理是否支持它们。

**可编辑内容：** 此处可编辑模型的答案（时间段列表）、推理以及提示词的应当/不应当规则。您的所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
steps: Play the clip and note which phases appear and roughly when they change.
Read the model's answer (the segment list with times and descriptions) and check the segments are correct, contiguous and non-overlapping, with none invented or missing.

Read the model's reasoning (L1 enumerates the phases in order, L2 justifies each boundary by a visible transition cue, L3 a one-line headline per segment), then open the 'Prompt rules' panel and check it obeys the DO / DON'T.

Rate every question below and add comments where they help. You can edit the ANSWER (segments, times, descriptions), the reasoning, and the DO / DON'T text directly ('Reset to original' undoes). Every edit is saved with your response.
steps_zh: 播放片段，留意出现了哪些阶段以及它们大致何时切换。
阅读模型的答案（含各时间段及其时间和描述的列表），检查各时间段是否正确、连续且不重叠，没有臆造或遗漏。
阅读模型的推理（L1 按顺序枚举各阶段，L2 通过可见的转换线索论证每个边界，L3 为每段给出一句标题），然后展开「提示词规则」面板，检查其是否遵守应当/不应当。
对下方每个问题评分并在需要时添加备注。您可以直接编辑【答案】（时间段列表、时间、描述）、推理以及应当/不应当文本（点击「恢复原文」可撤销）。所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。

VS.context:
**Video Summarization (VS).** The model watched this clip and was asked to *summarize the whole procedure* in a short paragraph (no per-segment timestamps), then justify it with a three-level reasoning trace. You are validating whether the **summary is faithful, complete and well-formed**, and whether the reasoning supports it.

**What you can edit:** the model's answer (the summary), its reasoning, and the prompt's DO/DON'T rules are all editable here. Every edit you make is saved with your response.
VS.context_zh:
**视频总结（VS）。** 模型观看了该片段，并被要求用一段简短文字*总结整个手术过程*（不含逐段时间戳），并用三层推理加以论证。您需要验证**总结是否忠实、完整、表述恰当**，以及推理是否支持它。

**可编辑内容：** 此处可编辑模型的答案（总结）、推理以及提示词的应当/不应当规则。您的所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。
steps: Play the whole clip to get its overall arc.
Read the model's summary (the answer) and check it is factually correct, covers the main surgical activities, omits nothing important, and invents nothing.

Read the model's reasoning (L1 surveys the arc, L2 separates the salient actions from minor/repeated ones, L3 compresses to the final summary), then open the 'Prompt rules' panel and check it obeys the DO / DON'T.

Rate every question below and add comments where they help. You can edit the SUMMARY (the answer), the reasoning, and the DO / DON'T text directly ('Reset to original' undoes). Every edit is saved with your response.
steps_zh: 完整播放片段，了解其整体过程。
阅读模型的总结（答案），检查其是否事实正确、涵盖主要手术操作、无重要遗漏、且无臆造。
阅读模型的推理（L1 梳理整体过程，L2 将显著操作与次要/重复操作区分开，L3 压缩为最终总结），然后展开「提示词规则」面板，检查其是否遵守应当/不应当。
对下方每个问题评分并在需要时添加备注。您可以直接编辑【总结】（答案）、推理以及应当/不应当文本（点击「恢复原文」可撤销）。所有编辑都会随评审结果一并保存。

dimensions.label: Reasoning <think> is faithful to the video (no invented detail)
L1 is correct (see the pinned levels for this task)
L3 is correct, and the final conclusion follows from it

items.question: You are an expert surgical analyst specializing in minimally invasive procedures. This video shows an endoscopic view of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a keyhole surgery to remove the gallbladder. The video may contain the following phases: preparation, carlot-triangle-dissection, clipping-and-cutting, gallbladder-dissection, gallbladder-packaging, cleaning-and-coagulation, gallbladder-extraction. When does gallbladder-packaging happen?
items.think:
L1 (phase signature & onset): Gallbladder-packaging is identified by graspers unfolding an endoscopic specimen_bag on the liver surface, opening the translucent pouch, placing the detached gallbladder inside, and cinching the drawstring; this becomes visible at the start of the clip around 0.0 seconds.

L2 (boundary contrast): No preceding operative phase is visible before this packaging activity, while the endpoint is fixed by the shift after the bag is closed and drawn toward the trocar: at about 98.0 seconds the graspers instead retract the liver and work on the cauterized gallbladder fossa with cleaning/coagulation contact.

L3 (phase-order bound & conclusion): In the sequence preparation -> Calot-triangle-dissection -> clipping-and-cutting -> gallbladder-dissection -> gallbladder-packaging -> cleaning-and-coagulation -> gallbladder-extraction, the bagging of the detached gallbladder immediately before liver-bed cleaning confirms gallbladder-packaging spans 0.0-97.0 seconds.

items.question: You are a specialist in analyzing minimally invasive surgery videos. This recording shows a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a procedure to remove the gallbladder through small abdominal incisions. The procedure may involve: preparation, carlot-triangle-dissection, clipping-and-cutting, gallbladder-dissection, gallbladder-packaging, cleaning-and-coagulation, gallbladder-extraction. Find the segment(s) where gallbladder-packaging happens.
items.think:
L1 (phase signature & onset): Gallbladder-packaging is first visible at 0.0 s, marked by a grasper retracting the liver while another grasper positions a specimen_bag over the liver and guides the detached gallbladder into it.

L2 (boundary contrast): No earlier phase activity is visible before this packaging onset; the phase ends when bagging/placement stops and the view changes to cleaning-and-coagulation, with graspers retracting the liver bed/fossa and tissue around the cystic duct region rather than manipulating the specimen_bag.

L3 (phase-order bound & conclusion): In the fixed order, gallbladder-packaging follows gallbladder-dissection and precedes cleaning-and-coagulation, matching the observed transition from bagging the detached gallbladder to cleaning the cauterized liver bed. Therefore, gallbladder-packaging occurs from 0.0-37.0 seconds.

items.question: You are an expert in surgical video interpretation. The following clip depicts a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a minimally invasive operation for gallbladder removal, captured via an endoscopic camera. Phases that may appear in the video are: preparation, carlot-triangle-dissection, clipping-and-cutting, gallbladder-dissection, gallbladder-packaging, cleaning-and-coagulation, gallbladder-extraction. When can I see gallbladder-dissection in the video?
items.think:
L1 (phase signature & onset): Gallbladder-dissection is visible from 0.0 s, with a grasper retracting and rotating the gallbladder while a hook coagulates and peels fibrous/peritoneal attachments along the gallbladder–liver bed interface.

L2 (boundary contrast): There is no preceding observed phase before this dissection activity, and the phase ends when hook-based liver-bed separation stops and the instruments switch to graspers manipulating a specimen_bag, cinching its cord, and placing the detached gallbladder into the bag at 241.0 s.

L3 (phase-order bound & conclusion): In the operative order after clipping-and-cutting and before gallbladder-packaging, the observed liver-bed peeling corresponds to gallbladder-dissection, followed by packaging with the specimen_bag. Therefore, gallbladder-dissection spans 0.0–239.0 seconds.

items.question: You are a specialist in analyzing minimally invasive surgery videos. This recording shows a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a procedure to remove the gallbladder through small abdominal incisions. Considering these surgical phases: ['preparation', 'carlot-triangle-dissection', 'clipping-and-cutting', 'gallbladder-dissection', 'gallbladder-packaging', 'cleaning-and-coagulation', 'gallbladder-extraction'], what is the most likely next phase?
items.think:
L1: At the clip’s end, the current phase is cleaning-and-coagulation: graspers retract/elevate the liver while the instrument tip works along the cauterized gallbladder fossa and adjacent clipped tissue to clear/coagulate the liver bed.

L2: The gallbladder is already detached and packed/cinched inside the specimen_bag, and the exposed liver bed has been repeatedly inspected and treated, with the instruments shifting from the fossa back toward the bag.

L3: In the fixed laparoscopic cholecystectomy sequence, once cleaning-and-coagulation has essentially cleared the fossa after the gallbladder is bagged, the next procedural action is removal of the bagged specimen; therefore the next step is gallbladder-extraction.

items.question: You are a specialist in analyzing minimally invasive surgery videos. This recording shows a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a procedure to remove the gallbladder through small abdominal incisions. What is the next surgical phase after the current video, given the phase list: ['preparation', 'carlot-triangle-dissection', 'clipping-and-cutting', 'gallbladder-dissection', 'gallbladder-packaging', 'cleaning-and-coagulation', 'gallbladder-extraction']?
items.think:
L1: At the clip’s end, the grasper is retracting the liver and manipulating tissue around the cauterized gallbladder fossa/liver bed, with irrigation/smoke clearing and no further Calot triangle dissection or gallbladder detachment, indicating cleaning-and-coagulation is in progress.

L2: The liver bed appears exposed and cauterized, the fossa has been cleared enough to reveal adjacent fatty tissue, and the white retrieval bag is visible, consistent with the gallbladder already freed and packed.

L3: In the fixed laparoscopic cholecystectomy sequence, once cleaning-and-coagulation after packaging is essentially complete, the next phase is removal of the bagged specimen; therefore the next step is gallbladder-extraction.

items.question: You are an expert surgical analyst specializing in minimally invasive procedures. This video shows an endoscopic view of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a keyhole surgery to remove the gallbladder. Based on the phase candidates: ['preparation', 'carlot-triangle-dissection', 'clipping-and-cutting', 'gallbladder-dissection', 'gallbladder-packaging', 'cleaning-and-coagulation', 'gallbladder-extraction'], predict the next step after this video.
items.think:
L1: At the clip’s end, the phase in progress is gallbladder-packaging: graspers manipulate the specimen_bag, guide the detached gallbladder into it, and draw the white cord to cinch the bag mouth while the liver is retracted.

L2: The packaging objective is essentially achieved because the gallbladder is already freed from the liver bed/fossa and enclosed within a partially closed specimen_bag, with the bag being shifted along the liver surface rather than further dissected.

L3: In the laparoscopic cholecystectomy sequence, after gallbladder-packaging the operative field is typically inspected, cleared, and hemostasis is addressed; therefore the next step is cleaning-and-coagulation.

items.question: You are an expert in surgical video interpretation. The following clip depicts a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a minimally invasive operation for gallbladder removal, captured via an endoscopic camera. Create an event list restricted to actions in ['preparation', 'carlot-triangle-dissection', 'clipping-and-cutting', 'gallbladder-dissection', 'gallbladder-packaging', 'cleaning-and-coagulation', 'gallbladder-extraction']. For each event: (1) start_time, (2) end_time, (3) description of this event.
items.answer:
0.0-97.0 seconds: After brief dark/occluded views, graspers manipulate the folded specimen_bag on the liver surface by repositioning, unfolding, and spreading it into an open translucent pouch, then lift the detached gallbladder into the bag and pack it down, grasp the rim and drawstring to fold and cinch the specimen_bag closed around the gallbladder against the liver surface, and finally draw the partially collapsed bag toward the trocar opening while a grasper retracts the liver to expose the adjacent dissected gallbladder bed.
98.0-170.0 seconds: Graspers retract and elevate the liver to maintain and widen exposure of the moist, bloody, cauterized gallbladder fossa, repeatedly repositioning along the raw liver bed margins while the instrument tip contacts the liver edge and adjacent clipped tissue for cleaning/coagulation, before moving over the liver surface and grasping the specimen_bag.
items.think:
L1 (scan & enumerate): The clip shows Gallbladder Packaging first: graspers unfold a specimen_bag on the liver, load the detached gallbladder, and cinch the bag. It then moves into Cleaning/Coagulation: graspers retract the liver and instruments contact the bloody cauterized gallbladder fossa/liver bed for cleaning or hemostasis.

L2 (boundaries): The boundary at 97.0–98.0s is fixed when the action changes from closing and drawing the specimen_bag toward the trocar to deliberate liver elevation and exposure of the raw gallbladder fossa. This makes the packaging segment end before fossa-focused cleaning/coagulation begins, keeping the two segments contiguous and non-overlapping.

L3 (per-segment content & conclusion): [0.0, 97.0] Gallbladder Packaging — graspers open/cinch specimen_bag around detached gallbladder; [98.0, 170.0] Cleaning/Coagulation — graspers retract liver while instrument cleans/coagulates gallbladder fossa.

items.question: You are an expert in surgical video interpretation. The following clip depicts a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a minimally invasive operation for gallbladder removal, captured via an endoscopic camera. Create an event list restricted to actions in ['preparation', 'carlot-triangle-dissection', 'clipping-and-cutting', 'gallbladder-dissection', 'gallbladder-packaging', 'cleaning-and-coagulation', 'gallbladder-extraction']. For each event: (1) start_time, (2) end_time, (3) description of this event.
items.answer:
0.0-37.0 seconds: The gallbladder-packaging phase begins with grasper retraction of the liver to expose the gallbladder bed, followed by graspers opening and positioning the specimen_bag over the liver as the detached gallbladder is lifted, guided, and placed into the bag.
38.0-132.0 seconds: During the cleaning-and-coagulation phase, a grasper repeatedly retracts the liver to expose and widen the blood-stained, cauterized gallbladder fossa/liver bed, moving from the central superior bed toward the inferior-right edge while additional graspers grasp the cystic duct region and apply traction on dissected tissue near the liver bed, with brief irrigation/smoke obscuration clearing to reveal adjacent fatty tissue and a white retrieval bag before the view abruptly becomes black.
items.think:
L1 (scan & enumerate): From 0.0–37.0s, gallbladder packaging appears: graspers retract the liver, open/position a specimen bag, and guide the detached gallbladder into it. From 38.0–132.0s, cleaning and coagulation appears: graspers retract the liver and apply traction around the cystic duct region while the cauterized gallbladder fossa/liver bed is exposed and cleared.

L2 (boundaries): The boundary at 37.0/38.0s is fixed by the action changing from specimen-bag placement and gallbladder insertion to repeated liver-bed exposure and cleaning/traction, making the phases non-overlapping. The second segment then continues contiguously through the liver-bed inspection/clearing until the view turns black at 132.0s.

L3 (per-segment content & conclusion): [0.0, 37.0] Gallbladder packaging — graspers place detached gallbladder into specimen bag over liver. [38.0, 132.0] Cleaning and coagulation — grasper retraction exposes cauterized gallbladder fossa and cystic duct region.

items.question: You are an expert in operative video analysis. The following video contains endoscopic footage of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, where the gallbladder is removed using small incisions and specialized instruments. Detect and caption only clinically relevant actions from ['preparation', 'carlot-triangle-dissection', 'clipping-and-cutting', 'gallbladder-dissection', 'gallbladder-packaging', 'cleaning-and-coagulation', 'gallbladder-extraction']. Return start/end times with a brief, objective description.
items.answer:
0.0-239.0 seconds: During gallbladder-dissection, the grasper repeatedly retracts, rotates, and lifts the gallbladder superiorly and laterally away from the liver undersurface to tension the peritoneal and fibrous attachments, while the hook instrument coagulates and dissects along the exposed yellow-gray/pale gallbladder–liver interface with brief cautery marks and smoke, progressively peeling the gallbladder from the liver bed, widening the visible dissection plane, and exposing the remaining adhesions for continued separation.
241.0-358.0 seconds: Graspers open and manipulate the specimen_bag, guide the detached gallbladder into it, grasp the rim and draw the white cord into a loop to cinch and collapse the bag mouth around the specimen, then shift the partially closed specimen_bag along the liver surface while a grasper retracts the liver.
items.think:
L1 (scan & enumerate): From 0.0–239.0s, gallbladder-dissection appears: the grasper retracts and lifts the gallbladder while the hook coagulates/dissects the gallbladder–liver bed plane. From 241.0–358.0s, gallbladder-packaging appears: graspers manipulate the specimen_bag, place the detached gallbladder inside, and cinch the bag.

L2 (boundaries): The transition is fixed between 239.0s and 241.0s by the change from hook cautery/dissection along the liver fossa to specimen_bag handling and drawstring closure, making the two intervals ordered, non-overlapping, and consecutive in the clip.

L3 (per-segment content & conclusion):  
[0.0, 239.0] — Gallbladder-dissection: hook separates gallbladder from liver bed under grasper traction.  
[241.0, 358.0] — Gallbladder-packaging: graspers bag and cinch the detached gallbladder.

items.question: You are a specialist in analyzing minimally invasive surgery videos. This recording shows a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a procedure to remove the gallbladder through small abdominal incisions. Summarize the video content in a short paragraph, focusing on the main surgical activities.
items.answer: graspers unfold a translucent specimen_bag on the liver surface, open it into a pouch, and place the detached gallbladder inside. The bag is folded and cinched closed by manipulating the rim and drawstring, then drawn toward the trocar while the liver is retracted. After bag retrieval begins, graspers elevate and reposition the liver to expose the moist, cauterized gallbladder fossa, with instrument contact along the raw bed and clipped tissue for cleaning/coagulation before returning to the specimen_bag.
items.think:
L1: The clip moves from gallbladder packaging to early extraction, with graspers unfolding a translucent specimen_bag on the liver, placing the detached gallbladder inside, cinching the rim and drawstring, and drawing the bag toward the trocar. It then transitions into cleaning/coagulation as graspers elevate the liver and expose the cauterized gallbladder fossa.

L2: The salient flow is bag opening, gallbladder insertion, bag closure, and traction toward removal; repeated grasping, rim adjustments, and liver retraction are minor supporting maneuvers. Once the bag is partly controlled, attention shifts back to the liver bed and adjacent clipped hepatocystic/Calot triangle tissue for exposure and contact cleaning/coagulation.

L3: Overall, the surgeon completes gallbladder packaging and begins specimen retrieval, then rechecks the operative field. The final progression is from secured specimen_bag handling to inspection and cleaning/coagulation of the moist, raw liver bed/fossa around the clipped cystic duct and cystic artery region before returning to the bag.

items.question: You are an expert surgical video reviewer. This endoscopic footage captures the steps of a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a minimally invasive gallbladder removal surgery. Summarize the video content in a short paragraph, focusing on the main surgical activities.
items.answer: A grasper retracts the liver to expose the gallbladder bed while the specimen bag is opened and positioned over the liver. The detached gallbladder is lifted, guided, and placed into the bag. The view then shifts to cleaning and coagulation of the blood-stained gallbladder fossa, with grasper traction widening exposure from the central superior bed toward the inferior-right edge. Additional graspers hold the cystic duct region and nearby dissected tissue as brief irrigation or smoke clearing reveals adjacent fatty tissue and the retrieval bag before the image cuts to black.
items.think:
L1 (survey the arc): The clip begins in the gallbladder packaging phase, with a grasper retracting the liver while the specimen bag is opened over the liver and the detached gallbladder is guided into it. It then transitions into cleaning and coagulation of the gallbladder fossa/liver bed.

L2 (salience & transitions): The defining transition is from specimen containment to post-dissection fossa management, shifting attention from the bagged gallbladder to the blood-stained, cauterized liver bed. Repeated grasper retraction of the liver and traction on the cystic duct-region tissue widen exposure, while brief irrigation or smoke clearing and glimpses of fatty tissue and the retrieval bag are secondary details.

L3 (compress & conclusion): Overall, the video progresses from specimen retrieval preparation to inspection and cleaning of the gallbladder bed. A grasper retracts the liver as the specimen bag is positioned and the gallbladder is placed inside, then graspers expose the fossa from the central superior bed toward the inferior-right edge while holding nearby dissected tissue before the view cuts to black.

items.question: You are an expert in surgical video interpretation. The following clip depicts a laparoscopic cholecystectomy, a minimally invasive operation for gallbladder removal, captured via an endoscopic camera. Give a concise overview of what the surgical video is about, without going into detailed steps.
items.answer: The grasper retracts and rotates the gallbladder superiorly and laterally to expose the gallbladder–liver interface. The hook coagulates and dissects along this plane, peeling the gallbladder off the liver bed and clearing remaining fibrous attachments. After detachment, graspers open the specimen_bag, place the gallbladder inside, and pull the drawstring to close the bag around the specimen. The partially closed specimen_bag is then repositioned along the liver surface while the liver is retracted.
items.think:
L1 (survey the arc): The clip moves from gallbladder-dissection, with the grasper retracting the gallbladder and the hook working along the gallbladder–liver interface, into gallbladder packaging after the gallbladder has been freed. The later phase shows graspers managing a specimen_bag, enclosing the gallbladder, and repositioning the partially closed bag near the liver.

L2 (salience & transitions): The key transition is from hook-based coagulation and dissection of fibrous attachments in the liver bed/fossa to specimen_bag handling once separation is complete. Repeated lifting, rotation, smoke, and small cautery marks are compressed as continued exposure and peeling of the gallbladder from the liver bed, while bag opening, rim grasping, and drawstring pulling define packaging.

L3 (compress & conclusion): The grasper retracts and rotates the gallbladder superiorly and laterally to expose the gallbladder–liver plane while the hook coagulates and dissects the remaining attachments from the liver bed. After detachment, the graspers open the specimen_bag, place the gallbladder inside, cinch the drawstring, and move the partially closed bag along the liver surface while the liver is retracted.
